diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: cityServed - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | cityServed | "Aarhus, Denmark")

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a single
    fluent, complex sentence. Unknown predicates fall back to a generic phrasing.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes if present."""
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Helper to build a clause for a single triple
    def _clause(s: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
        """
        Produce a natural‑language clause for a single triple.
        The function now covers a broader set of predicates and uses
        more fluent phrasing for runway information.
        """
        # Basic geographic / administrative predicates
        if p == "cityServed":
            return f"{s} serves the city of {o}"
        if p == "location":
            return f"{s} is located in {o}"
        if p == "country":
            return f"{s} is situated in {o}"
        if p == "isPartOf":
            return f"{s} is part of {o}"
        if p in ("elevationAboveTheSeaLevel", "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres"):
            return f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level"
        if p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
            return f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level"
        if p == "operatingOrganisation":
            return f"{s} is operated by {o}"
        # Codes and identifiers
        if p == "iataLocationIdentifier":
            return f"{s} has the IATA code {o}"
        if p == "icaoLocationIdentifier":
            return f"{s} has the ICAO code {o}"
        if p == "locationIdentifier":
            return f"{s} uses the location identifier {o}"
        # Runway related predicates – handle numbers, names, lengths, surfaces
        if p.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is designated {o}"
        if p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is {o} feet long"
        if p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is {o} metres long"
        if p.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway surface is {o}"
        if p == "runwayName":
            return f"{s}'s runway is named {o}"
        if p == "runwayLength":
            return f"{s}'s runway length is {o}"
        if p == "runwaySurfaceType":
            return f"{s} has a runway surface of {o}"
        # Ownership / administrative details
        if p == "owner":
            return f"{s} is owned by {o}"
        if p == "largestCity":
            return f"The largest city of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "capital":
            return f"The capital of {s} is {o}"
        if p in ("language", "officialLanguage"):
            return f"The official language of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "leader":
            return f"The leader of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "leaderTitle":
            return f"The leader title of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "leaderParty":
            return f"The leader of {s} belongs to the {o} party"
        # Miscellaneous aviation‑related predicates
        if p == "regionServed":
            return f"{s} serves the region of {o}"
        if p == "hubAirport":
            return f"{s} uses {o} as its hub airport"
        if p == "aircraftHelicopter":
            return f"{s} operates the helicopter {o}"
        if p == "transportAircraft":
            return f"{s} operates the transport aircraft {o}"
        if p == "aircraftFighter":
            return f"{s} operates the fighter aircraft {o}"
        if p == "attackAircraft":
            return f"{s} operates the attack aircraft {o}"
        if p == "battle":
            return f"{s} participated in the battle of {o}"
        if p == "nativeName":
            return f"The native name of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "foundingYear":
            return f"{s} was founded in {o}"
        if p == "founder" or p == "foundedBy":
            return f"{s} was founded by {o}"
        if p == "postalCode":
            return f"The postal code of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "areaCode":
            return f"The area code of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "ceremonialCounty":
            return f"{s} lies in the ceremonial county of {o}"
        if p == "jurisdiction":
            return f"{s} falls under the jurisdiction of {o}"
        if p == "demonym":
            return f"The demonym for {s} is {o}"
        # Fallback for any other predicate – use a generic but readable phrasing
        return f"{s} has {p.replace('_', ' ')} {o}"

    # Build clauses
    raw_clauses = [_clause(t.subject, t.predicate, _clean(t.object)) for t in triples]
    clauses = raw_clauses

    # Combine clauses into a single complex sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
